scope: AgenC agents/mailbox.ts ↔ codex codex-rs/core/src/agent/mailbox.rs strict parity (with documented Node-platform adaptations: bounded queue + agent_exited sentinel + bidirectional direction tag)

requiredBehaviors: InterAgentCommunication carries author, recipient, content, triggerTurn matching codex protocol shape
AgenC adds direction (up/down) for bidirectional routing — documented adaptation for I-5
AgenC adds metadata field as a typed extension surface for interrupt reason / task id
Mailbox class combines codex's Mailbox + MailboxReceiver into a single owner (single-threaded JS adaptation)
monotonically increasing seq numbers (codex Mailbox::send returns u64 seq)
BehaviorSubject seqWatch replays current seq + delivers next on subscribe (matches codex tokio::watch semantics)
I-31: agent_exited sentinel emitted exactly once after close()
I-64 non-blocking synchronous send
I-16 bounded with overflow salvage window (Node-specific adaptation; codex uses unbounded mpsc)
throws MailboxClosedError after close() so spurious sends surface immediately
advances seq + notifies seqWatch under all accepted paths
non-mutating peek; true iff queued items exist OR a closed mailbox still owes the agent_exited sentinel
matches codex MailboxReceiver::has_pending semantics (sync_pending_mails + queue check)
true iff any queued message has triggerTurn=true
matches codex's MailboxReceiver::has_pending_trigger_turn
removes + returns all queued items in FIFO delivery order
promotes the overflow salvage slot before returning so a successful drain wins the salvage race (I-16)
emits the agent_exited sentinel exactly once on the first drain after close (I-31); subsequent drains return empty
close() is idempotent
I-31: AgentExitedSentinel emitted from drain exactly once after close, never again
subsequent send() throws MailboxClosedError (AgenC closure semantic; codex has no closed-mailbox concept)
overflow timer cleared on close so it cannot fire after close
AsyncIterable yields new arrivals as they land
terminates after agent_exited sentinel emit (matches codex's tokio watch+drain pattern that ends when the channel closes)
yields items in FIFO order; observers don't miss a queued message that lands during a yield gap
